RULING: DEFENDANT BRAGG INVESTMENT COMPANY, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
No appearance by moving party and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Bragg Investment Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Mr. Wait's deposition testimony precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. The Court did not consider defendant's reply papers because the papers were untimely filed under C.C.P. 1005(b). Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok and J. Alameda. Not reported. (503/CML)

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted as amended: Plaintiffs' motion for order granting preference in setting case for trial, and extending discovery cutoff is denied without prejudice. Plaintiffs failed to demonstrate that Mr. Wait's health is such that preference is necessary to prevent prejudicing his interest in the litigation, as required by C.C.P. 36(a). The Court further ordered advanced the June 21, 2018 Trial Setting Conference to December 21, 2017. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Reporter: David A. RULING:
PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF

Pursuant to defendant Superior Boiler Works, Inc.'s request, the Court continued plaintiffs' motion for order granting preference in setting case for trial, and extending discovery cutoff to Tuesday, October 31, 2017 at 9:30 a.m. in Department 503. The Court allowed defendant Superior Boiler to submit its courtesy copies of the opposition to the courtroom before the end of the day. The Court further determined that if the motion granted at the next hearing, the last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) would be February 23, 2018. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, matter not reported (503/CML)
